laws.title: Public Law 23-172 | Congressional Military Service Act
laws.status_basis: Claude's archived disposition classifies the enactment as repealed, so no current Code text is authorized even though the recovered source contains direct amendments and repeals.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate-effective-date language.
provisions.evidence: The source includes the act's opening clauses, but no current Code operation is authorized.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 18-140 section 304(a) to revise enlistment and commissioning rules.
provisions.exact_change: Do not create live Code text from this repealed enactment.
provisions.evidence: The source directs a replacement of section 304(a) of PL 18-140.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 10 U.S.C. 12204 and rewrites 10 U.S.C. 12203.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only the repeal history; do not apply the withdrawn text as current law.
provisions.evidence: The source states that section 12204 is repealed and section 12203 is rewritten.

provisions.text_summary: Rewrites 10 U.S.C. 151 and adds uncodified protections and limitations.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve only as repeal history; no live section text should be created.
provisions.evidence: The source rewrites section 151 and adds ancillary protections that are not current Code text.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML preserves the enactment as history rather than as live Code text, which matches the repealed disposition.

recommended_actions: Retain the enactment only as a historical note.
Do not promote any of the repealed or rewritten sections into current Code text.
Remove the Trello link if it remains attached.

laws.title: Public Law 24-173 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source is a general and permanent foreign-affairs statute with no express Code citation; Claude preserved it as a statutory note rather than a positive-law amendment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The act's opening section contains the general clauses and definitions.

provisions.text_summary: Bars federal employees from holding positions in foreign governments.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory note only.
provisions.evidence: The section prohibits federal employees from holding foreign-government posts.

provisions.text_summary: Bars elected officials from foreign-government or foreign-military employment.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory note only.
provisions.evidence: The section prohibits elected officials from foreign-government or foreign-military employment.

provisions.text_summary: Extends the foreign-position bar to Senate-confirmed officials.
provisions.exact_change: Preserve as statutory note only.
provisions.evidence: The section extends the same rule to Senate-confirmed officials.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML already treats the enactment as a national-security statutory note; no unique positive-law section was determinable.

recommended_actions: Retain a concise statutory note.
Do not invent a permanent Code section.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 24-174 | Washington, D.C. Permitless Carry Act
laws.status_basis: Claude's archived disposition classifies the enactment as repealed, so it is retained only as statutory history and not as current Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, purpose, and definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as history only.
provisions.evidence: The source identifies the act as a District of Columbia carry measure.

provisions.text_summary: Eligibility and location restrictions for permitless carry in D.C.
provisions.exact_change: Do not elevate to permanent Code text.
provisions.evidence: The source sets eligibility criteria and location restrictions for carry in D.C.

provisions.text_summary: Special rules for Congress, judges, law enforcement, parks, and enforcement.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as historical note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source adds special rules and enforcement provisions for the D.C. context.

provisions.text_summary: Rights restoration, supremacy clause, and Secret Service identification rules.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as historical note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source includes a restoration clause, a supremacy clause, and identification rules.
provisions.notes: Source numbering includes duplicate section labels.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML keeps the act as a District of Columbia statutory note, which is the right general treatment for a repealed local enactment.

recommended_actions: Keep only as a concise D.C. historical note.
Do not promote to permanent positive-law text.
Remove the Trello link and verify any superseded labeling separately.

laws.title: Public Law 24-178 | Equality in Employment Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source expressly amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation, and Claude's later-law record treats the substantive change as already incorporated.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, preamble, and findings.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as note material only.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections introduce the act and contain findings rather than direct Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Amends 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a) to add gender identity and sexual orientation.
provisions.exact_change: Add gender identity and sexual orientation to the protected characteristics in subsection (a).
provisions.evidence: Section 6(a) expressly directs amendment of 42 U.S.C. 2000e-2(a).
provisions.notes: The substantive amendment is already present in the XML.

provisions.text_summary: EEOC and Labor implementation directives.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source directs agency enforcement and rulemaking.

provisions.text_summary: Conclusion and sense language.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only as conclusion text.
provisions.evidence: The closing section is a concluding sense statement rather than positive-law text.

current_implementation.detail: The Title 42 amendment is present, but the codification note is mis-anchored to Title 29 and still carries a full-text dump that should be removed.

recommended_actions: Keep the Title 42 amendment as applied text.
Remove the full-text dump and Trello link from the note.
Move or replace the note at the correct Title 42 anchor.
Retain the EEOC and Labor directives as concise statutory notes.

laws.title: Public Law 25-179 | Fiscal Year 21-2
laws.status_basis: The recovered enactment is a temporary appropriations law with a short fiscal window, so it belongs as a statutory note rather than permanent Code text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and immediate-effective-date rule.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as introductory note material only.
provisions.evidence: The opening sections give the title and immediate-effect clause.

provisions.text_summary: Appropriations and reporting provisions.
provisions.exact_change: Exclude appropriations from permanent Code text.
provisions.evidence: The act appropriates funds for the stated short fiscal period.

provisions.text_summary: Temporary COLA suspension under 2 U.S.C. 4501.
provisions.exact_change: Record the temporary suspension as a note only.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 expressly suspends the COLA adjustment for the fiscal year.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is an acceptable appropriations-history anchor, but the act itself is temporary and should not be treated as permanent Code text.

recommended_actions: Keep only a concise historical note.
Exclude appropriations from permanent Code text.
Record the temporary section 5 note at 2 U.S.C. 4501.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 25-180 | Sanction Repealment Act
laws.status_basis: Claude's archived disposition marks the enactment as repealed, but the recovered source still matters because it expressly repeals PL 9-55 and nullifies the sanctions regime on Iran.

provisions.text_summary: Short title and purpose.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: The opening clauses identify the repealing statute and its purpose.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 9-55 in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Record repeal-marking at the original law's home.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(b) expressly repeals Public Law 9-55 in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Declares sanctions on Iran null and void.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the sanctions-nullification language as a short note only.
provisions.evidence: Section 3(c) declares all sanctions on Iran null and void.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as standard note material only.
provisions.evidence: The source includes severability and immediate-effect language.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML note is a reasonable foreign-relations anchor, but the repeal of PL 9-55 should also be recorded at the original Code home.

recommended_actions: Record the PL 9-55 repeal as a repeal-marking at its original Code home.
Keep the sanctions-nullification language as a concise statutory note.
Remove the Trello link.
